elements.title: Ada Cambridge
elements.contents: ... that despite being the wife of a priest, Ada Cambridge (pictured) was a fierce critic of organised religion?

elements.title: a cache in the tomb
elements.contents: ... that in 1914 archaeologists discovered a cache in the tomb of Sithathoriunet that ancient tomb robbers had missed?

elements.title: Tiny Jesus
elements.contents: ... that Tiny Jesus figurines have appeared in cities across the world?

elements.title: algorithmic amplification
elements.contents: ... that a 2021 investigation found that algorithmic amplification on TikTok could steer users towards self-harm content within hours?

elements.title: Kaneza Schaal
elements.contents: ... that Kaneza Schaal and Christopher Myers created Cartography after seeing a young boy playing music?

elements.title: Hanna's Town
elements.contents: ... that Hanna's Town was the site of the first English courts west of the Allegheny Mountains?

elements.title: Atzimba Casas
elements.contents: ... that Atzimba Casas was inspired to play football after watching the anime Captain Tsubasa?

elements.title: New Zealand Bill of Rights Act 1990
elements.contents: ... that the New Zealand Bill of Rights Act 1990 has been described as "one of the weakest affirmations of human rights" in the world because courts cannot strike down inconsistent laws?

elements.title: Silvius Leopold Weiss
elements.contents: ... that a vengeful violinist tried to bite off parts of the thumb of lutenist Silvius Leopold Weiss, but failed to end his career?
